content:
{
  "lens": "security",
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "critical",
      "category": "crypto",
      "file": "src/auth/auth.service.ts",
      "line": 7,
      "title": "bcrypt cost factor 4 is brute-forceable in seconds",
      "description": "SALT_ROUNDS is set to 4, which produces a hash in ~0.5ms. OWASP recommends a minimum cost factor of 12 (producing a hash in ~250ms). At cost 4, an attacker with a GPU can test billions of passwords per second.",
      "suggestion": "Set SALT_ROUNDS to at least 12. Consider making it configurable via environment variable with 12 as the floor.",
      "confidence": 0.98
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "auth",
      "file": "src/middleware/auth.middleware.ts",
      "line": 20,
      "title": "JWT verification accepts algorithm 'none' and ignores expiry",
      "description": "The jwt.verify call uses ignoreExpiration: true and does not restrict allowed algorithms. The 'alg: none' attack allows forging tokens without a secret. Ignoring expiry means stolen tokens work forever.",
      "suggestion": "Set algorithms: ['HS256'] explicitly and remove ignoreExpiration (or set it to false). Consider short-lived tokens (15min) with refresh token rotation.",
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "input-validation",
      "file": "src/auth/auth.controller.ts",
      "line": 10,
      "title": "No input validation on request body",
      "description": "The register and login endpoints accept any req.body without validation. An extremely long password (>1MB) sent to bcrypt.hash will cause CPU exhaustion (algorithmic DoS). Missing email format validation allows garbage data in the database.",
      "suggestion": "Validate input with a schema library (zod, joi). Enforce max password length of 72 bytes (bcrypt truncation limit) and validate email format.",
      "confidence": 0.88
    }
  ]
}
